title: Sabanka
summary: Q: identify John Zodrow's Sabanka parish. Evidence favors Lebehnke, but Sabanka, Johann Grams grave, and 1868 Allemannia Zodrow all fail; next pull Lebehnke files and land. Primary added 2026-07-08: the 1885 Kansas state census places the household in Republic Co (Elk Creek) — the Sheridan Co move came after 1885 — with Rosa Grams (~80) living in it, corroborating her 1895 death at son-in-law John Zodrow's residence.

title: Zimmerman/Zinkle Mainhardt
summary: Q: test Mainhardt parents for John Paul Zimmerman/Catherine Zinkle. Mainhardt has zero Zimmermann/Zink* in 205 births and FS locality is indexer inference; next pull Archion or US Catholic Heimat.

title: Leonard->Michael + Doyle obit
summary: Q: harden Leonard-to-Michael and find Doyle's Apr 2005 obit. Memorials support the chain but CFP skips 5-20 Apr; next pull obit elsewhere or Leonard primary proof.

title: Nauer/Koeberger
summary: Q: prove Thomas Nauer's parents and begin Koeberger. The 1851 census documents a Prussia-born Catholic Nauer/Wand household in Wilmot Township, but Thomas is absent and relationships are unstated. The cluster is real; Thomas's link and the Meyer/Wand naming remain open. Next pull his 1856 marriage and parish records.

title: Rust/Adolph/Strawn
summary: Q: are Frances Rust/Adolph/Strawn one woman? The 1940 and 1950 Thomas County censuses place Evelyn and Herbert with the same grandparents and reconcile Lon A. with Albert L. Adolph; Mary F. Adolph is Mary Frances Rust. Rust→Adolph is resolved. The later Strawn marriage still needs a primary record.

title: Rust parents
summary: RESOLVED 2026-07-09: the 1900, 1905, and 1910 censuses preserve the same Francis, Clarence, and William household, and the widowed 1910 Etta Rust household directly identifies Francis as her daughter while placing Walter Mundell there in the couple's marriage year. John F. Rust and Ethel Rupert are Mary Frances's parents. The open question is now upstream: do records prove John F. as the son of John Aissen Rust and Frauke Folkerts Everwyn? The separate 1830 burial and 1841 marriage belong to older Jan Aissen and Aisse Jansen Rust, not John Aissen born in 1842.
source_note: 1900+1905+1910 KS census Smith Co; Wybelsum parish

title: Marjorie Clemans
summary: Q: identify Marjorie Evelyn Clemans's parents. The 1933 Washington Co marriage record and the 1939 Colorado divorce (which gives her full name, Marjorie Evelyn Mundell) confirm her as Evelyn's mother, an adult b. ~1912. Four same-name candidates are now excluded: Barron Co WI (Talmadge Clemans's daughter, b. ~1922); Marjorie Opal of Liberty Mills IN (b. 1914, m. Stamper); Marjorie E. of Kit Carson Co CO (b. 1918, dau. Laura Clemans); Marjorie Emily of Waynoka OK (b. 1915, dau. Benjamin F. Clemans, m. Weaver). The one traceable eastern-CO Clemans family (Robert M. Clemans of Flagler; sons Earl and Jasper William of Merino/Logan Co) was reconstructed 1900–1940 and does NOT contain her: Robert M. widowed with children raised by grandparents by 1900, Earl a childless newlywed in 1920, Jasper's only documented child a daughter by his second wife (Jasper's ~1909 first marriage is an undocumented gap). No Marjorie Clemans in any CO/KS census 1920–40; no grave/death/remarriage located. Origin unrecovered online; next paths are offline CO vital records or her later-life documents.
source_note: 1933 CO marriage Washington Co no.1896; CO Divorce Index 1939

title: Leora Young/Rogers
summary: Q: explain Leora/William Rogers. The 1950 Knob Hill census identifies Jerry, Walter, Donald, and a daughter as Homer and Leora's children, making the named sons Evelyn's half-brothers; Herbert remained with the Adolph grandparents. No Leora Mundell remarriage appears in the Colorado index. Rogers remains open outside that coverage.
source_note: 1950 census Knob Hill; Colorado marriage index

title: Cantwell maternal line
summary: Q: secure Sarah Alice Cantwell's maternal line. 1888 Henry bio supports Thomas/Jemima, but Sarah Alice-to-Barnabas and Farmer need parent/household records.

title: Catherine Moore
summary: Q: identify Catherine Moore and her parents. The 1860 census supports a Pennsylvania birth about 1839; her daughter's 1886 application and a 1901 son's biography confirm Catherine as Zephaniah's wife and mother. Death, burial, and parents remain open. Next pull Zephaniah's later marriage, then church or probate records.

title: Dible immigrant layer
summary: Q: identify Jacob Dible's immigrant layer. Dreibelbis 1761/DeVilbiss 1777 wills disconfirm grafts; next pull the deeper Westmoreland deeds. ORDER SETTLED 2026-07-08: John A. Dible's 1866 Westmoreland (Penn Twp) will, probate case 2663, names eleven children including Zephaniah — John Albert is Zephaniah's father, confirming the chart's placement; the will's widow is Rebecah (a later wife; Catherine Heckman earlier).
source_note: 1866 John A. Dible will, Westmoreland Co PA Wills case 2663; DeVilbiss/PA Archives

title: Haden/Reed
summary: Q: prove Haden/Reed beyond Kay Haden. Christ Church proves Moseley vitals, not Jenny's Haden marriage, and King William burned; next pull deeds, probate, bonds, household proof.

title: Long Gen 5-6
summary: Q: harden Long/Sleight/McClelland/Love Gen 5-6. Chapman, 1757 will, and [1764] deed stand, but Samuel-to-Robert/Pearl Twp need probate, land, Bible proof.

title: James Connelly parents
summary: Q: who were James Connelly's parents? James + America works through cemetery/sibling evidence, but no parent set surfaced; next pull death, probate, land, census, church.

title: Durham one-or-two
summary: Q: one David Monroe Durham or two? WKU supports 1832-1897 Nebo man and America link, while child pages add Jr./1829-1887; next pull originals.

title: Ford father/date
summary: Q: was John Laborn Ford's father John Sr. or Levi, and marriage 1840 or 1842? 1911 son letter supports John Sr./Apr 1840; later leads raise Levi/1842; next pull deeds, estates, marriage.

title: Hoyle vs. Hogle
summary: Q: was Samuel Claar's bride Hoyle, Hogle, or Hayle? The county index fixes 5 Oct 1895 at Oberlin and reads Hogle. The failed database search was a Decatur County coverage gap, not an Iowa clue. Only the original record can settle the spelling.

title: wedding date
summary: Verdict: Doyle/Evelyn married 14 Jun 1954; CFP 2004 anniversary beats copied 1953 text.

title: Jule vs. Julius
summary: Verdict: Doyle's middle name is Julius; "Jule" is memorial wording and the marker abbreviates Doyle J.

title: Long generation slip
summary: Verdict: Rev. Robert Long is Robert Nelson Long's grandfather through John Franklin Long, not his father.

title: Cecilia parent correction
summary: CLOSED 2026-07-08: the 1910 US Census (Sheridan Co, Kansas, ED 0187, sheet 6A) enumerates "Celia A", age 5, as daughter of Leonard [indexed "Farrow", an enumerator misreading of Zodrow] and Katie, with siblings Leo J (10) and Mary M (8) — the exact sibling set from the memorial grids (the 1920 census corroborates, Cecilia age 15 in the same household). A federal record thus places Cecilia in Leonard Ferdinand Zodrow's household, not Julius's; the compiled FamilySearch indexing that made Julius + Ernestina her parents is superseded.
source_note: 1910 census NARA T624 roll 454 ED 0187 sheet 6A; Cecilia/Leonard memorials

title: Mundell origin
summary: Q: prove Harvey as a son of John Mundell, then extend through James. The official 1837 probate index names John as James's son, corroborated by a will transcript. Harvey-to-John remains open; the original probate file is still wanted.

title: Catherine Fleckenstein parents
summary: Q: identify Catherine Fleckenstein's parents without reopening the completed Cecilia correction. The 1910 and 1920 Zodrow households establish Catherine as Cecilia's mother but do not name Catherine's own parents; next pull Cecilia's 1904 Leoville baptism, Leonard and Catherine's marriage record, and Catherine's death or parish records.
